channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran Apologises to Gulf Neighbours Amid Ongoing US-Israel Strikes and Trump Threats
item.description: The Middle East conflict involving Iran, the United States, and Israel has entered its second week, with both sides exchanging intense attacks. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian issued a rare apolog...

item.title: The link between gut microbes and cardiovascular health
item.description: Recent research highlights the significant role the gut microbiome plays in cardiovascular health. The gut is home to a variety of microbes that influence digestion, immune function, metabolism, and e...

item.title: First Lady Opens Modern Elderly Centre in Akwa Ibom, Urges Law to Support Senior Citizens
item.description: For Ikono Local Government Area, Akwa Ibom State, Nigerian First Lady, Senator Oluremi Tinubu, don officially open one correct Geriatric home wey dem call ‘Oluremi Tinubu Senior Citizens Center’. Duri...

item.title: Indian Navy may be deployed to assist stranded ships in Persian Gulf amid regional tensions
item.description: The Indian government is evaluating the deployment of Indian Navy vessels to escort merchant ships stranded in the Persian Gulf due to regional tensions. A senior government source has stated that a f...

item.title: Ford issues recalls for 1.74 million vehicles in the US due to rearview camera faults
item.description: Ford has announced two separate recalls in the United States affecting a total of approximately 1.74 million vehicles because of issues with their rearview camera systems, which could potentially heig...

item.title: Government Faces Criticism from Allies and Opposition Over Rs55 Fuel Price Hike
item.description: On 8th March 2026, Pakistan’s government came under severe criticism from both opposition parties and some of its own allies following a sudden Rs55 per litre increase in petrol and diesel prices, ann...

item.title: Pakistan Faces Oil Price Hike Amid Middle East Crisis Despite Existing Reserves
item.description: KARACHI: Pakistan has felt the first direct economic shock from the ongoing Middle East conflict as the government raised petrol and diesel prices by Rs55 per litre, despite having 28-day old oil stoc...

item.title: Ambassador Stephen Mbundi becomes new EAC Secretary General
item.description: Ambassador Stephen Mbundi from Tanzania has been appointed as the new Secretary General of the East African Community (EAC) during the 25th Summit of EAC Heads of State in Arusha on March 7, 2026. He ...

item.title: Trump says U.S. can manage Iran conflict without British support
item.description: President Donald Trump stated that the United States does not require assistance from the United Kingdom to manage the ongoing conflict with Iran, despite reports that Britain is considering sending t...

item.title: Rising Living Costs Threaten Food Security for Vulnerable Australian Families
item.description: Amid rising living costs and ongoing global conflicts, food insecurity in Australia is becoming an increasing concern. Charities like Sydney Community Connect are providing free food hampers to suppor...

item.title: President Murmu Raises Concerns Over Venue Change for Santal Conference in West Bengal
item.description: President Droupadi Murmu visited Darjeeling district in West Bengal to participate in the ninth International Santal Conference. The event, initially planned at a large field in Bidhannagar, was shift...

item.title: From Student Activism to County Governorship: A Personal Account
item.description: The article shares the personal story of an individual who rose from turbulent student days at university to becoming a county governor in Kenya. It begins with the author's experiences amid campus ch...

item.title: Court of Appeals orders release of Chinese national over drug case evidence issues
item.description: The Court of Appeals (CA) has directed the release of Xu Zhou, also known as Wang, a Chinese national previously convicted for possession and sale of over 90 kilos of shabu in 2024. The appellate cour...

item.title: List of 10 Enduring Colonial-Era Buildings and Bridges in Nigeria: Their History, Current State and Preservation Risks
item.description: Nigeria get plenty colonial-era structures wey don survive more than one hundred years, even with all the urban growth, heavy traffic, and weather wahala. This article list 10 of them, from Lagos to K...

item.title: DA demands suspension of health DG pending corruption investigation
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has called for the suspension of Health Director-General Sandile Buthelezi amid investigations into alleged corruption and fraud. Buthelezi, along with two senior official...

item.title: Six-year-old deaf boy and family deported from the US, unable to access hearing aid
item.description: A six-year-old deaf boy, along with his younger brother and mother, has been deported from the United States to Colombia after being detained during a routine check-in at an ICE office in San Francisc...

item.title: Lagos Government Warns Actress Sarah Martins Against Cooking Charity Meals on Public Roads
item.description: The Lagos State Government don warn Nollywood actress Sarah Martins say she no suppose dey cook food for public roads, walkways or medians again for her charity outreach. This one come after she post ...

item.title: Machine Learning Identifies Key Indicator of Fast Ion Movement in Solid-State Batteries
item.description: Researchers have developed a machine learning approach that could significantly advance the development of all-solid-state batteries (ASSBs), which are safer and potentially more energy-dense than con...

item.title: Experts Analyse Likely Scenarios in Middle East Conflict Following Iran Strikes
item.description: One week into the ongoing Middle East conflict, experts are warning of a protracted and unpredictable situation following the US and Israeli strikes that killed Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Kh...

item.title: Zone 2 Police Command Celebrates Second Place Finish with 246 Medals at BIPOGA 2026
item.description: Zone 2 Command of the Nigeria Police Force (NPF) proudly received their athletes back at the Zonal Headquarters in Onikan, Lagos, after an impressive performance at the 15th Biennial Police Games (BIP...
